Based on our review, I concur entirely with the established understanding of iron deficiency anemia markers. High TIBC indicates excess protein seeking out iron, low transferrin saturation shows insufficient iron-binding capability relative to demand, while lowered ferritin signals diminished storage iron levels—all consistent across options A-D.

Therefore, **my final chosen answer remains: Option B**, encompassing TIBC, Ferritin, and Transferrin Saturation. Agreeing fully with the consensus reached earlier. No disagreement exists within these choices under normal interpretation standards. My selected answer does not change.
